articles.headline: India and Canada Announce Landmark Economic Partnership Deal to Revitalize Bilateral Ties
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney jointly announced a comprehensive economic partnership agreement.
• The deal aims to significantly boost trade and investment across key sectors, including critical minerals, clean energy, technology, and agriculture.
• This agreement marks a crucial step in normalizing and strengthening relations after two years of strained diplomatic ties.
• Joint working groups will be established to oversee the implementation and identify new areas for collaboration.
• The partnership is expected to open new markets for Indian exports and attract Canadian investment into India's growing economy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Prime Ministers: Narendra Modi (India), Mark Carney (Canada).
Key sectors of cooperation: critical minerals, clean energy, technology, agriculture.
Aim: Strengthen economic partnership, boost trade, and mend diplomatic ties.
Announcement date: March 2, 2026.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of this deal for India's 'Act East' policy and its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the potential economic benefits for both India and Canada, including diversification of trade partners and supply chains.
Examine how this agreement addresses the challenges of strained diplomatic relations and lays a foundation for future cooperation.
Evaluate the role of critical minerals and clean energy cooperation in India's energy security and climate change goals.
Critically assess the implications for the Indian diaspora in Canada and their role in enhancing bilateral ties.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney shaking hands after signing the economic partnership agreement.
articles.trivia: Canada is one of the world's largest producers of several critical minerals, including potash, uranium, and nickel, crucial for India's green energy transition.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Digital Privacy in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment reinforcing the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age.
• The ruling emphasized the need for robust data protection mechanisms and stricter government oversight on data collection and usage.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, aligning with global best practices.
• The judgment arose from petitions challenging specific provisions of existing surveillance frameworks and data retention policies.
• Experts laud the decision as a significant step towards safeguarding individual liberties and promoting responsible technological governance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court bench: Headed by Chief Justice of India.
Key aspects: Reaffirms Right to Privacy (Article 21), calls for comprehensive data protection law.
Context: Petitions challenging surveillance frameworks.
Significance: Impact on government data policies and corporate data handling.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of this judgment on existing government surveillance programs and the balance between national security and individual privacy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in enacting a robust data protection law that balances innovation with privacy concerns.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in protecting fundamental rights in the context of rapid technological advancements.
Critically examine the concept of 'digital sovereignty' and how this ruling contributes to it.
Suggest reforms needed in India's legal framework to align with global standards on data privacy.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a lock icon over digital data, symbolizing data protection and privacy.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet gave its nod to the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, with an enhanced outlay of ₹25,000 crore.
• Phase II focuses on scaling up green hydrogen production, developing a robust supply chain, and promoting export opportunities.
• Key initiatives include establishing green hydrogen corridors, incentivizing indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers, and funding R&D projects.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• This phase builds upon the initial success of pilot projects and aims for commercial viability and widespread adoption.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched EOS-08, an advanced Earth Observation Satellite, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The satellite, carried by the PSLV-C58 rocket, is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads.
• EOS-08 will provide critical data for urban planning, disaster management, agricultural monitoring, and resource mapping.
• This mission underscores India's growing capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for national development.
• The data from EOS-08 is expected to significantly enhance India's geospatial intelligence capabilities.

articles.trivia: ISRO has one of the world's largest constellations of remote sensing satellites, providing crucial data for various applications.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies, focusing on fairness, accountability, transparency, and safety.
• It proposes guidelines for data privacy, algorithmic bias detection, human oversight, and explainable AI across various sectors.
• The policy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving industry, academia, civil society, and government in its implementation.
• This move positions India as a leader in ethical AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being and regulatory prudence.

Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.

Evaluate the challenges in implementing a multi-stakeholder approach to AI governance in a diverse country like India.
Examine the potential for India to emerge as a global leader in responsible AI development and its implications for international cooperation.
Critically assess the balance between regulatory oversight and promoting a vibrant AI startup ecosystem.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Citing Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was driven by concerns over persistent core inflation and geopolitical uncertainties impacting global commodity prices.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted upside risks from food inflation and crude oil prices.
• The move signals a cautious approach to monetary policy, prioritizing price stability amidst evolving economic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision.
Repo rate: Maintained at 6.5%.
Reason: Persistent core inflation, geopolitical uncertainties.
RBI Governor: Shaktikanta Das.
Outlook: Cautious approach, focus on price stability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing economic growth with inflation control in the current global economic scenario.
Analyze the impact of the unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures, particularly food and energy prices.
Examine the role of global geopolitical events and crude oil prices in shaping India's domestic inflation trajectory.
Critically assess the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance of the RBI and its implications for future interest rate movements.
articles.imageDescription: RBI headquarters building in Mumbai with a graph showing economic trends.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme for Holistic Rural Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development launched the 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme, a new initiative aimed at holistic rural development.
• The scheme integrates various existing programs to focus on livelihood enhancement, infrastructure development, and social security in rural areas.
• Key components include skill development for rural youth, promotion of local enterprises, and improved access to basic amenities like water and sanitation.
• It emphasizes a decentralized approach, empowering Gram Panchayats to plan and implement development projects tailored to local needs.
• 'Gram Samriddhi' seeks to reduce rural-urban disparities and foster self-reliant villages across India.

articles.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi famously advocated for 'Gram Swaraj' or village self-rule, envisioning self-sufficient and self-governing village republics.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, Focus on Digital Public Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, bringing together delegates from member nations to discuss future digital cooperation.
• The key agenda focused on promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), ensuring digital inclusion, and fostering secure digital payments systems.
• Union Minister for Electronics & IT highlighted India's success with Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker as examples of scalable DPIs.
• Discussions also revolved around addressing digital divides, cybersecurity challenges, and establishing common frameworks for cross-border data flows.
• The meeting concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing collaboration for a universally accessible, secure, and inclusive digital future.

articles.headline: Report Highlights India's Continued Growth in Renewable Energy Capacity
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) showcased India's significant strides in expanding its renewable energy capacity.
• India's total installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) has crossed 200 GW, making it one of the top global leaders.
• The report emphasized strong growth in solar and wind power, driven by supportive government policies and private sector investment.
• It also highlighted the role of renewable energy in reducing carbon emissions and enhancing India's energy independence.
• Challenges such as grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition for projects were also addressed, with proposed mitigation strategies.

articles.headline: Latest Tiger Census Shows Steady Growth in India's Big Cat Population
articles.explainer:
• The quadrennial All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) report for 2025 was released, indicating a steady increase in India's tiger population.
• The population estimate now stands at approximately 3,800-4,000 tigers, up from previous counts, showcasing successful conservation efforts.
• Key tiger reserves in Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka, and Uttarakhand showed robust growth, attributed to enhanced protection and habitat management.
• The report also highlighted challenges such as human-wildlife conflict, poaching, and habitat fragmentation in certain regions.
• India continues to be home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, reinforcing its commitment to tiger conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Report: All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) for 2025.

India's share: Over 70% of world's wild tiger population.
mainsPoints: Discuss the success story of Project Tiger and India's leadership in global tiger conservation efforts.
Analyze the factors contributing to the increase in tiger population, including effective anti-poaching measures and habitat restoration.
Examine the persistent challenges in tiger conservation, particularly human-wildlife conflict and the need for sustainable coexistence strategies.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in successful wildlife conservation programs.
Suggest innovative approaches and policy reforms to address habitat fragmentation and ensure long-term viability of tiger populations.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage.
articles.trivia: The 'Stripe Pattern Recognition Software' is used in tiger censuses to identify individual tigers, as each tiger has a unique stripe pattern.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms in Police Act, Focus on Accountability
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending comprehensive amendments to the existing Police Act.
• The report calls for a modern, citizen-centric police force with enhanced accountability and transparency mechanisms.
• Key recommendations include separating law and order from investigation, establishing independent police complaints authorities, and improving police welfare.
• It also emphasized the need for better training, technological upgradation, and gender sensitization within the police force.
• The proposed reforms aim to address long-standing issues of political interference, outdated practices, and public trust deficit in policing.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Harappan Site in Gujarat
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant new Harappan civilization site in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• Preliminary excavations revealed remnants of a well-planned urban settlement, pottery, tools, and intricate beadwork dating back to 2500-1900 BCE.
• The site provides fresh insights into the geographical spread and cultural diversity of the Mature Harappan period.
• Researchers believe the discovery could shed more light on trade routes and interactions between different Harappan settlements.
• This finding is expected to contribute substantially to our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization and its decline.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.

Significance: Insights into geographical spread, trade, and culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries like this one in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the unique features of Harappan urban planning and their relevance to modern city development.
Evaluate the methods used by archaeologists to date and interpret findings from ancient sites and their limitations.
Examine how trade and cultural exchange played a role in the prosperity and decline of the Harappan civilization.

articles.imageDescription: An artist's rendition of a bustling Harappan city, with well-structured buildings and a public bath.
articles.trivia: Dholavira, also in Gujarat, is one of the five largest Harappan sites and is known for its advanced water management system.

articles.headline: India Signs MoU with African Nations for Enhanced Agricultural Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with a consortium of African nations to strengthen cooperation in agriculture and food security.
• The agreement focuses on knowledge transfer, technological assistance, capacity building, and joint research in sustainable farming practices.
• Areas of collaboration include crop diversification, water management, post-harvest technologies, and agricultural mechanization.
• This initiative is part of India's broader commitment to South-South cooperation and enhancing food resilience in developing countries.
• The partnership aims to boost agricultural productivity, improve farmer incomes, and address food insecurity in both regions.

• These drone swarms are designed for surveillance, reconnaissance, and precision strike capabilities in contested environments.
• The move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, promoting self-reliance in defence manufacturing and cutting-edge technology.
• Indian startups and MSMEs have been instrumental in developing these sophisticated drone technologies.
• This acquisition is expected to significantly enhance the Army's operational capabilities and modernize its warfare tactics.

articles.headline: High-Level Meeting Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A high-level review meeting, chaired by the Union Education Minister, assessed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• The review focused on curriculum reforms, teacher training programs, digital education initiatives, and skill development integration.
• States presented their progress reports, highlighting both achievements and challenges in adapting the policy to local contexts.
• Special attention was given to foundational literacy and numeracy, vocational education, and equitable access to quality education.

articles.headline: Urban Development Ministry Releases Smart Cities Mission Progress Report

• Key successes include integrated command and control centers, smart mobility solutions, and improved waste management systems.
• Challenges such as funding gaps, project delays, and citizen participation were also acknowledged, with remedial measures outlined.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Launches Nationwide Campaign to Combat Non-Communicable Diseases

• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare initiated a comprehensive nationwide campaign focusing on the prevention and control of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign targets major NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and various cancers, which contribute significantly to the disease burden.
• It emphasizes lifestyle modifications, regular health screenings, early detection, and access to affordable treatment.
• Public awareness programs, community health worker training, and integration of NCD services into primary healthcare are key pillars.
• The initiative aims to reduce premature mortality from NCDs and improve the overall health outcomes of the population.

articles.headline: Border Security Forces Enhance Surveillance and Patrolling Along Western Front
articles.explainer:
• Border Security Forces (BSF) intensified surveillance and patrolling operations along India's western front with Pakistan.
• The heightened alert follows intelligence inputs regarding increased infiltration attempts and cross-border illicit activities.
• Advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors, have been deployed to monitor vulnerable areas.
• The BSF is also collaborating closely with local police and intelligence agencies to strengthen the security grid.
• This proactive measure aims to thwart any inimical designs and maintain peace and stability in the sensitive border regions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Forces involved: Border Security Forces (BSF).
Region: India's western front (with Pakistan).
Reason: Increased infiltration attempts, illicit activities.
Technologies deployed: Drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors.
Collaboration: Local police, intelligence agencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the persistent security challenges faced by India along its western border and the strategies employed to counter them.
Analyze the role of advanced surveillance technologies in enhancing border security and preventing cross-border infiltration and smuggling.
Evaluate the effectiveness of integrated border management systems in creating a robust security grid.
Examine the importance of coordination between central armed police forces, state police, and intelligence agencies in border area security.
Critically assess the socio-economic implications of heightened security measures on border populations and their livelihoods.
articles.imageDescription: BSF personnel patrolling a border fence at sunrise, symbolizing vigilance.
articles.trivia: The Border Security Force (BSF) is India's primary border guarding force on its borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates Mega Solar Park in Rajasthan, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity
articles.explainer:
• A Union Minister inaugurated a new 1,500 MW mega solar park in Rajasthan, significantly adding to India's renewable energy capacity.
• The park, developed under the Solar Park Scheme, leverages Rajasthan's high solar insolation and vast barren lands.
• It is expected to power over a million households and offset a substantial amount of carbon emissions annually.
• The project involved public-private partnerships and created numerous local employment opportunities.
• This initiative reinforces India's commitment to achieving its ambitious clean energy targets and promoting sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Inauguration: 1,500 MW mega solar park.
Location: Rajasthan.
Scheme: Solar Park Scheme.
Capacity: 1,500 MW.
Benefits: Powering households, carbon emission offset, job creation.
Context: India's clean energy targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of states like Rajasthan in leading India's solar energy revolution due to their geographical advantages.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing large-scale solar parks for India's energy security and climate goals.
Evaluate the challenges associated with land acquisition, grid integration, and water management for mega solar projects.
Examine the role of government schemes and public-private partnerships in accelerating the deployment of renewable energy infrastructure.
Critically assess the impact of such projects on local communities and the importance of inclusive development models.
articles.imageDescription: Vast expanse of solar panels under a clear blue sky in a desert landscape.
articles.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest fully commissioned solar photovoltaic parks.

articles.headline: Government Expands Digital Health Services to Remote Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced the expansion of digital health services to cover more remote rural areas across India.
• This initiative leverages telemedicine platforms, e-prescription services, and digital health records to improve access to healthcare.
• Health and Wellness Centers are being equipped with necessary digital infrastructure and trained personnel.
• The aim is to bridge the urban-rural divide in healthcare access and provide timely medical consultations and follow-ups.
• This push for digital health is part of the broader Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to create a robust national digital health ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Initiative by: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Focus: Expansion of digital health services to rural areas.
Services: Telemedicine, e-prescription, digital health records.
Infrastructure: Health and Wellness Centers.
Parent mission: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of digital health technologies in bridging the urban-rural healthcare divide in India.
Analyze the key components of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) and its role in creating a unified national digital health ecosystem.
Evaluate the benefits and challenges of implementing telemedicine and e-prescription services in remote rural settings.
Examine the importance of digital literacy and robust data privacy measures for the successful adoption of digital health services.
Critically assess how digital health solutions can improve health outcomes, reduce healthcare costs, and enhance disease surveillance.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor consulting a patient via a tablet screen in a rural setting, symbolizing telemedicine.
articles.trivia: India is home to the world's largest government-funded healthcare scheme, Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY).

articles.headline: IMD Predicts Normal Monsoon for 2026, Relief for Agricultural Sector
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) released its initial forecast, predicting a 'normal' monsoon for the 2026 season.
• This forecast brings relief to the agricultural sector, which heavily relies on timely and adequate rainfall for crop production.
• IMD cited favorable global weather patterns, including the absence of strong El Niño conditions, as key factors.
• The announcement is crucial for government planning regarding agricultural policies, water management, and food security.
• While a normal monsoon is predicted, regional variations and the impact of climate change on rainfall patterns remain areas of concern.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Forecast by: India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Prediction: 'Normal' monsoon for 2026 season.
Key factor: Favorable global weather patterns, no strong El Niño.
Significance: Relief for agricultural sector, impacts government planning.
Concerns: Regional variations, climate change impact.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical importance of the Southwest Monsoon for India's agricultural economy and overall food security.
Analyze the various global and regional factors that influence the Indian monsoon, including El Niño and the Indian Ocean Dipole.
Evaluate the advancements in monsoon forecasting technology and the challenges in predicting regional rainfall patterns accurately.
Examine the government's strategies and policies to mitigate the risks associated with monsoon variability for farmers.
Critically assess the long-term impact of climate change on India's monsoon patterns and the need for adaptive agricultural practices.
articles.imageDescription: Dark monsoon clouds gathering over lush green agricultural fields.
articles.trivia: The term 'monsoon' is derived from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning 'season'.

highlights: India and Canada sign a landmark economic partnership deal, aiming to boost trade and mend strained diplomatic ties.
Supreme Court reinforces digital privacy rights, urging government to fast-track comprehensive data protection law.
Union Cabinet approves Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission with ₹25,000 crore outlay to scale production and promote exports.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08, enhancing capabilities for urban planning and disaster management.
Latest Tiger Census reveals steady growth in India's tiger population, reaching approximately 3,800-4,000, signifying successful conservation efforts.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent India-Canada economic partnership deal, consider the following statements:
1. The deal primarily focuses on cooperation in critical minerals, clean energy, and technology.
2. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the agreement.
3. The agreement aims to address a period of strained diplomatic relations between the two countries.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The deal focuses on critical sectors, was announced by both Prime Ministers, and aims to strengthen ties after a period of strained relations.

prelimsMocks.question:
The National Green Hydrogen Mission, recently approved for Phase II, primarily aims to:
1. Promote the use of grey hydrogen in industrial applications.
2. Scale up green hydrogen production and establish supply chains.
3. Incentivize the indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers.
4. Focus solely on domestic consumption, restricting exports.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Green Hydrogen Mission focuses on green hydrogen, not grey. It aims to scale production, establish supply chains, and incentivize electrolyzer manufacturing. It also promotes export opportunities, not restricts them. Thus, statements 2 and 3 are correct.

prelimsMocks.question:
EOS-08, recently launched by ISRO, is an advanced Earth Observation Satellite. Which of the following are its primary applications?
1. Providing data for urban planning.
2. Assisting in disaster management.
3. Monitoring agricultural health.
4. Enhancing deep space exploration capabilities.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: EOS-08 is an Earth Observation Satellite, primarily used for applications related to Earth's surface and atmosphere, such as urban planning, disaster management, and agricultural monitoring. Deep space exploration is a different type of mission. Hence, statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the latest All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) report for 2025:
1. The report indicates a significant decline in India's tiger population.
2. India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
3. Human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation remain challenges for tiger conservation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as the report shows a steady increase in the tiger population. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, highlighting India's significant share of the global tiger population and the ongoing challenges in conservation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is NOT a key recommendation of the Parliamentary Panel for reforms in the Police Act?
(a) Separation of law and order from investigation functions.
(b) Establishment of independent police complaints authorities.
(c) Increased political interference in daily policing operations.
(d) Improvement in police welfare and technological upgradation.
options: (a) Separation of law and order from investigation functions.
(b) Establishment of independent police complaints authorities.
(c) Increased political interference in daily policing operations.
(d) Improvement in police welfare and technological upgradation.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Increased political interference in daily policing operations.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Parliamentary Panel's recommendations aim to address and reduce political interference, not increase it. The other options (a, b, d) are indeed key recommendations for police reforms.

mainsMocks.question: The recent India-Canada economic partnership deal signifies a crucial step in India's economic diplomacy and bilateral relations. Analyze the strategic importance of this agreement for India, particularly in the context of its evolving foreign policy and energy security needs. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the deal and its context (strained ties). Discuss its strategic importance for economic diversification, access to critical minerals (energy security), and strengthening a key bilateral relationship. Link it to India's broader foreign policy objectives and global standing. Conclude with potential for future cooperation.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent ruling on digital privacy underscores the challenges of safeguarding fundamental rights in the age of rapid technological advancement. Discuss the implications of this judgment for the government's data collection policies and the ongoing efforts to enact a comprehensive data protection law in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the significance of the SC ruling and its reaffirmation of the Right to Privacy. Discuss how it impacts government surveillance and data practices, potentially requiring reforms. Elaborate on the urgency it creates for a data protection law, outlining key principles (consent, purpose limitation, data minimization) that such a law must embody. Address the balance between national security, innovation, and individual privacy. Conclude with the need for a robust, future-proof legal framework.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II aims to position the country as a global hub for green hydrogen. Evaluate the opportunities and challenges associated with achieving this ambition, and suggest policy measures to ensure the mission's success. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by defining green hydrogen and its importance for India's energy transition and climate goals. Discuss opportunities like abundant renewable resources, potential for export, and job creation. Address challenges such as high production costs, need for massive infrastructure, water availability, and technological advancements. Suggest policy measures including R&D incentives, PLI schemes for electrolyzers, green hydrogen corridors, international collaborations, and carbon pricing mechanisms. Conclude with the potential for India to lead in this emerging sector.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Harappan Site in Gujarat
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant new Harappan civilization site in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• Preliminary excavations revealed remnants of a well-planned urban settlement, pottery, tools, and intricate beadwork dating back to 2500-1900 BCE.
• The site provides fresh insights into the geographical spread and cultural diversity of the Mature Harappan period.
• Researchers believe the discovery could shed more light on trade routes and interactions between different Harappan settlements.
• This finding is expected to contribute substantially to our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization and its decline.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: Discovery by: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
Location: Kutch region, Gujarat.
Civilization: Harappan (Indus Valley Civilization).
Period: Mature Harappan (2500-1900 BCE).
Findings: Urban settlement, pottery, tools, beadwork.
Significance: Insights into geographical spread, trade, and culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries like this one in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the unique features of Harappan urban planning and their relevance to modern city development.
Evaluate the methods used by archaeologists to date and interpret findings from ancient sites and their limitations.
Examine how trade and cultural exchange played a role in the prosperity and decline of the Harappan civilization.
Discuss the challenges in preserving newly discovered archaeological sites amidst development pressures and environmental factors.
GS1.imageDescription: An artist's rendition of a bustling Harappan city, with well-structured buildings and a public bath.
GS1.trivia: Dholavira, also in Gujarat, is one of the five largest Harappan sites and is known for its advanced water management system.

GS2.headline: India and Canada Announce Landmark Economic Partnership Deal to Revitalize Bilateral Ties
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney jointly announced a comprehensive economic partnership agreement.
• The deal aims to significantly boost trade and investment across key sectors, including critical minerals, clean energy, technology, and agriculture.
• This agreement marks a crucial step in normalizing and strengthening relations after two years of strained diplomatic ties.
• Joint working groups will be established to oversee the implementation and identify new areas for collaboration.
• The partnership is expected to open new markets for Indian exports and attract Canadian investment into India's growing economy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

Key sectors of cooperation: critical minerals, clean energy, technology, agriculture.
Aim: Strengthen economic partnership, boost trade, and mend diplomatic ties.
Announcement date: March 2, 2026.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of this deal for India's 'Act East' policy and its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the potential economic benefits for both India and Canada, including diversification of trade partners and supply chains.
Examine how this agreement addresses the challenges of strained diplomatic relations and lays a foundation for future cooperation.
Evaluate the role of critical minerals and clean energy cooperation in India's energy security and climate change goals.
Critically assess the implications for the Indian diaspora in Canada and their role in enhancing bilateral ties.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney shaking hands after signing the economic partnership agreement.
GS2.trivia: Canada is one of the world's largest producers of several critical minerals, including potash, uranium, and nickel, crucial for India's green energy transition.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Digital Privacy in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment reinforcing the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age.
• The ruling emphasized the need for robust data protection mechanisms and stricter government oversight on data collection and usage.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, aligning with global best practices.
• The judgment arose from petitions challenging specific provisions of existing surveillance frameworks and data retention policies.
• Experts laud the decision as a significant step towards safeguarding individual liberties and promoting responsible technological governance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court bench: Headed by Chief Justice of India.
Key aspects: Reaffirms Right to Privacy (Article 21), calls for comprehensive data protection law.
Context: Petitions challenging surveillance frameworks.
Significance: Impact on government data policies and corporate data handling.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of this judgment on existing government surveillance programs and the balance between national security and individual privacy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in enacting a robust data protection law that balances innovation with privacy concerns.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in protecting fundamental rights in the context of rapid technological advancements.
Critically examine the concept of 'digital sovereignty' and how this ruling contributes to it.
Suggest reforms needed in India's legal framework to align with global standards on data privacy.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a lock icon over digital data, symbolizing data protection and privacy.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme for Holistic Rural Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development launched the 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme, a new initiative aimed at holistic rural development.
• The scheme integrates various existing programs to focus on livelihood enhancement, infrastructure development, and social security in rural areas.
• Key components include skill development for rural youth, promotion of local enterprises, and improved access to basic amenities like water and sanitation.
• It emphasizes a decentralized approach, empowering Gram Panchayats to plan and implement development projects tailored to local needs.
• 'Gram Samriddhi' seeks to reduce rural-urban disparities and foster self-reliant villages across India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Scheme: Gram Samriddhi Scheme.
Launched by: Ministry of Rural Development.
Objective: Holistic rural development.
Focus areas: Livelihood, infrastructure, social security.
Approach: Decentralized, Gram Panchayat empowerment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for a holistic approach to rural development in India, considering the multi-faceted challenges faced by rural communities.
Analyze how the 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme aims to achieve synergy between existing rural welfare programs and improve their effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of Gram Panchayats in the successful implementation of such decentralized development schemes.
Examine the potential impact of this scheme on reducing rural poverty, enhancing employment opportunities, and improving quality of life in villages.
Critically assess the challenges in ensuring equitable distribution of benefits and preventing leakages in rural development initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: A vibrant village scene depicting community activities and improved infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi famously advocated for 'Gram Swaraj' or village self-rule, envisioning self-sufficient and self-governing village republics.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, Focus on Digital Public Infrastructure
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, bringing together delegates from member nations to discuss future digital cooperation.
• The key agenda focused on promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), ensuring digital inclusion, and fostering secure digital payments systems.
• Union Minister for Electronics & IT highlighted India's success with Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker as examples of scalable DPIs.
• Discussions also revolved around addressing digital divides, cybersecurity challenges, and establishing common frameworks for cross-border data flows.
• The meeting concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing collaboration for a universally accessible, secure, and inclusive digital future.

Key agenda: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), digital inclusion, secure digital payments.

mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) on a global stage, citing examples like UPI and Aadhaar.

GS2.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems, facilitating billions of transactions annually.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms in Police Act, Focus on Accountability
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending comprehensive amendments to the existing Police Act.
• The report calls for a modern, citizen-centric police force with enhanced accountability and transparency mechanisms.
• Key recommendations include separating law and order from investigation, establishing independent police complaints authorities, and improving police welfare.
• It also emphasized the need for better training, technological upgradation, and gender sensitization within the police force.
• The proposed reforms aim to address long-standing issues of political interference, outdated practices, and public trust deficit in policing.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

prelimsPoints: Report by: Parliamentary Standing Committee.
Focus: Amendments to Police Act, police reforms.
Key recommendations: Separate law & order from investigation, independent complaints authorities, police welfare, training, tech upgradation, gender sensitization.
Aim: Citizen-centric, accountable, transparent police force.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and persistent challenges necessitating police reforms in India, citing past committee recommendations.
Analyze how the proposed separation of law and order from investigation could enhance the efficiency and fairness of the criminal justice system.
Evaluate the role of independent police complaints authorities in ensuring accountability and addressing public grievances.
Examine the importance of police welfare, training, and technological upgradation in building a modern and effective police force.
Critically assess the political and administrative hurdles in implementing comprehensive police reforms across states in India.

GS2.trivia: The Police Act of 1861, enacted during British rule, still forms the basis of policing in India, despite numerous calls for its overhaul.

GS2.headline: India Signs MoU with African Nations for Enhanced Agricultural Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with a consortium of African nations to strengthen cooperation in agriculture and food security.
• The agreement focuses on knowledge transfer, technological assistance, capacity building, and joint research in sustainable farming practices.
• Areas of collaboration include crop diversification, water management, post-harvest technologies, and agricultural mechanization.
• This initiative is part of India's broader commitment to South-South cooperation and enhancing food resilience in developing countries.
• The partnership aims to boost agricultural productivity, improve farmer incomes, and address food insecurity in both regions.

GS2.headline: High-Level Meeting Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level review meeting, chaired by the Union Education Minister, assessed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• The review focused on curriculum reforms, teacher training programs, digital education initiatives, and skill development integration.
• States presented their progress reports, highlighting both achievements and challenges in adapting the policy to local contexts.
• Special attention was given to foundational literacy and numeracy, vocational education, and equitable access to quality education.
• The meeting concluded with a roadmap for accelerating implementation in critical areas and addressing bottlenecks.

Analyze the key challenges faced by states in implementing the NEP 2020, particularly concerning resource allocation and teacher capacity building.

GS2.headline: Urban Development Ministry Releases Smart Cities Mission Progress Report
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs released its annual progress report on the Smart Cities Mission (SCM).
• The report highlighted significant achievements in implementing various urban infrastructure and technology-driven projects across 100 Smart Cities.
• Key successes include integrated command and control centers, smart mobility solutions, and improved waste management systems.
• Challenges such as funding gaps, project delays, and citizen participation were also acknowledged, with remedial measures outlined.

GS2.headline: Health Ministry Launches Nationwide Campaign to Combat Non-Communicable Diseases
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare initiated a comprehensive nationwide campaign focusing on the prevention and control of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign targets major NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and various cancers, which contribute significantly to the disease burden.
• It emphasizes lifestyle modifications, regular health screenings, early detection, and access to affordable treatment.
• Public awareness programs, community health worker training, and integration of NCD services into primary healthcare are key pillars.
• The initiative aims to reduce premature mortality from NCDs and improve the overall health outcomes of the population.

GS2.headline: Government Expands Digital Health Services to Remote Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced the expansion of digital health services to cover more remote rural areas across India.
• This initiative leverages telemedicine platforms, e-prescription services, and digital health records to improve access to healthcare.
• Health and Wellness Centers are being equipped with necessary digital infrastructure and trained personnel.
• The aim is to bridge the urban-rural divide in healthcare access and provide timely medical consultations and follow-ups.
• This push for digital health is part of the broader Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to create a robust national digital health ecosystem.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet gave its nod to the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, with an enhanced outlay of ₹25,000 crore.
• Phase II focuses on scaling up green hydrogen production, developing a robust supply chain, and promoting export opportunities.
• Key initiatives include establishing green hydrogen corridors, incentivizing indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers, and funding R&D projects.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• This phase builds upon the initial success of pilot projects and aims for commercial viability and widespread adoption.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched EOS-08, an advanced Earth Observation Satellite, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The satellite, carried by the PSLV-C58 rocket, is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads.
• EOS-08 will provide critical data for urban planning, disaster management, agricultural monitoring, and resource mapping.
• This mission underscores India's growing capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for national development.
• The data from EOS-08 is expected to significantly enhance India's geospatial intelligence capabilities.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies, focusing on fairness, accountability, transparency, and safety.
• It proposes guidelines for data privacy, algorithmic bias detection, human oversight, and explainable AI across various sectors.
• The policy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving industry, academia, civil society, and government in its implementation.
• This move positions India as a leader in ethical AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being and regulatory prudence.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Citing Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was driven by concerns over persistent core inflation and geopolitical uncertainties impacting global commodity prices.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted upside risks from food inflation and crude oil prices.
• The move signals a cautious approach to monetary policy, prioritizing price stability amidst evolving economic conditions.

GS3.headline: Report Highlights India's Continued Growth in Renewable Energy Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• A new report released by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) showcased India's significant strides in expanding its renewable energy capacity.
• India's total installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) has crossed 200 GW, making it one of the top global leaders.
• The report emphasized strong growth in solar and wind power, driven by supportive government policies and private sector investment.
• It also highlighted the role of renewable energy in reducing carbon emissions and enhancing India's energy independence.
• Challenges such as grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition for projects were also addressed, with proposed mitigation strategies.

GS3.headline: Latest Tiger Census Shows Steady Growth in India's Big Cat Population
GS3.explainer:
• The quadrennial All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) report for 2025 was released, indicating a steady increase in India's tiger population.
• The population estimate now stands at approximately 3,800-4,000 tigers, up from previous counts, showcasing successful conservation efforts.
• Key tiger reserves in Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka, and Uttarakhand showed robust growth, attributed to enhanced protection and habitat management.
• The report also highlighted challenges such as human-wildlife conflict, poaching, and habitat fragmentation in certain regions.
• India continues to be home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, reinforcing its commitment to tiger conservation.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Sanctions Major Procurement of Indigenous Drone Swarms for Army
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence approved a significant procurement order for advanced indigenous drone swarms for the Indian Army.
• These drone swarms are designed for surveillance, reconnaissance, and precision strike capabilities in contested environments.
• The move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, promoting self-reliance in defence manufacturing and cutting-edge technology.
• Indian startups and MSMEs have been instrumental in developing these sophisticated drone technologies.

GS3.headline: Border Security Forces Enhance Surveillance and Patrolling Along Western Front
GS3.explainer:
• Border Security Forces (BSF) intensified surveillance and patrolling operations along India's western front with Pakistan.
• The heightened alert follows intelligence inputs regarding increased infiltration attempts and cross-border illicit activities.
• Advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors, have been deployed to monitor vulnerable areas.
• The BSF is also collaborating closely with local police and intelligence agencies to strengthen the security grid.
• This proactive measure aims to thwart any inimical designs and maintain peace and stability in the sensitive border regions.

GS3.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates Mega Solar Park in Rajasthan, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• A Union Minister inaugurated a new 1,500 MW mega solar park in Rajasthan, significantly adding to India's renewable energy capacity.
• The park, developed under the Solar Park Scheme, leverages Rajasthan's high solar insolation and vast barren lands.
• It is expected to power over a million households and offset a substantial amount of carbon emissions annually.
• The project involved public-private partnerships and created numerous local employment opportunities.
• This initiative reinforces India's commitment to achieving its ambitious clean energy targets and promoting sustainable development.
